cases.name: First-year nonissuer audit engagement letter with predecessor communication

intake.user_request: Generate first-year audit engagement letter for a private C-corp manufacturer in California. Prior auditor was a different firm. Engagement covers 12/31 year-end audit for the upcoming fiscal year.

expected_required_elements: Objective and scope (AU-C §210)
Auditor and management responsibilities
Framework identification
Form and content of audit report
Inherent limitations
Independence affirmation (AICPA Code §1.200)
Predecessor-auditor communication (AU-C §510)
Communication framework (AU-C §260)
Material weakness / significant deficiency communication (AU-C §265)
California state-overlay indemnification limitation (Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code §5063.1)
AICPA Ethics Interpretation §1.400.205 attest-indemnification limitation
Fee arrangement
Termination provisions

cases.name: Schedule C tax engagement bundled with bookkeeping (independence not relevant; not attest)

intake.user_request: Sole-proprietor consulting business client in Texas. Need engagement letter covering 2025 federal Form 1040 + Schedule C + Schedule SE + Texas franchise tax (no income tax in TX) + monthly bookkeeping. No attest. Firm has FTC Safeguards Rule program in place.

cases.expected_template: Combination of letters/tax-1040.md + letters/bookkeeping.md (two separate engagement letters recommended; cannot bundle unrelated services into single document with single fee structure)
expected_required_elements: Tax engagement: returns covered (1040 + Schedule C + Schedule SE + TX franchise + extensions)
Tax engagement: reliance-on-client-records disclaimer
Tax engagement: FTC Safeguards Rule (16 CFR Part 314) data-security clause
Tax engagement: §7216 disclosure consent for any third-party use
Tax engagement: NO contingent fees per AICPA Code §1.510
Bookkeeping engagement: scope (data entry, bank rec, GL maintenance)
Bookkeeping engagement: management retains responsibility for books
Bookkeeping engagement: §1.295 NOT required (client is not attest)
TX state overlay: 22 TAC §501.71 general contract law applies; reasonable limitation of liability acceptable for non-attest
Termination provisions
Out-of-scope: audit / review / compilation; multistate nexus expansion; investment advice

cases.name: SOC 1 Type 2 first-year examination of investment fund administrator with carve-out subservice

intake.user_request: Generate SOC 1 Type 2 engagement letter for a hedge-fund administrator in New York. 12-month reporting period 1/1-12/31. Subservice organizations: AWS (carve-out), Salesforce (carve-out), and a third-party reconciliation tool from a smaller vendor (inclusive method per the vendor's request). Multiple control objectives covering NAV calculation, trade processing, and corporate-action processing.

expected_required_elements: AT-C §320 standard reference (SOC 1 examination)
Type 2 (12-month period 1/1-12/31)
System description scope (NAV, trade processing, corporate actions)
Subservice treatment: carve-out for AWS + Salesforce; INCLUSIVE for the third-party reconciliation tool (third party provides assertion + auditor's report)
CSOCs (complementary subservice organization controls) for AWS + Salesforce identified
CUECs (complementary user entity controls) for fund-administrator client entities

Control objectives defined by management; reflect impact on user-entity (fund) ICFR — must be specific (e.g., 'NAV calculations are accurate and complete' tied to user-entity NAV-related F/S assertions)
Independence affirmation (AT-C §205 / Code §1.200)
Use restriction: management of administrator + user entities (funds) + auditors of user entities
AICPA Ethics Interpretation §1.400.205 attest-indemnification limitation
NY state-board overlay (8 NYCRR Subpart 70-1) for licensure
Fee, termination per common clauses
